commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 12. "Who is the man that feareth the Lord?" from which fear he begins to come to wisdom. "He shall establish a law for him in the way, which he hath chosen" (ver. 12). He shall establish a law for him in the way, which in his freedom he has taken, that he may not sin now with impunity.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
He hath. This may be understood either of God, or of man, who has chosen a state of life. (Berthier)
Provided he be guided by the fear of the Lord, (Calmet) all things will turn to his advantage. (Haydock)
